answer:
**No, these are not the same Bṛhaspati, nor the same work.** The *Bṛhaspati Smṛti* (Sanskrit; ancient) is a *dharmaśāstra* focused on legal procedure (*vyavahāra*), kingship, courts, disputes over property/debts, punishments for assault/theft/adultery, inheritance, and social order—topics central to worldly jurisprudence.[^1][^2][^3] It opens with the origins of lawsuits from greed/violence, lists 18 topics (e.g., debts, deposits, sales without title, boundary disputes), details court composition (king, assessors, scribes), proofs (witnesses/documents/ordeals), and rules for castes/regions.[^4][^5]

In stark contrast, the *Vṛhaspatitattva* (Kawi/Old Javanese; medieval) is a Śaiva philosophical dialogue between Śiva and Vṛhaspati on metaphysics (*tattva-śāstra*), cosmology, yoga, and liberation. Śiva expounds 36 *tattvas* (principles): *paramaśiva*, *sadāśiva*, *māyā*, *pradhāna*, *guṇas* (*sattva/rajas/tamas*), *buddhi*, *ahaṃkāra*, *indriyas*, *pañcamahābhūtas*, subtle body (*sūkṣmaśarīra*), vital airs (*prāṇas*), states of consciousness (*pañcapadas*), and *ṣaḍaṅgayoga* (*pratyāhāra* to *samādhi*) for realizing the divine.[^6][^7][^8] It culminates in *aṣṭaiśvaryas* (eight powers like *aṇimā*) and critiques material attachment.[^9]

No content overlap: the Smṛti has zero metaphysics/yoga; the Tattva has no law/courts/punishments. The shared name reflects mythic attribution—Bṛhaspati as divine guru/preceptor of gods—but spans eras/genres: ancient Indic law vs. medieval Javanese tantric ontology. Equivalent to crediting "Plato" for disparate works; traditional authorship is honorific, not identical.[^10]

[^2]: Ibid., [/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-d81171].

[^5]: Ibid., [/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-7409b9].
[^6]: *Vṛhaspatitattva*, Ch. 1, [/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-635a93].

[^10]: *Bṛhaspati Smṛti*, Ch. 1 (legal focus), [/works/unknown-brhaspati-smrti-0fd070/01/translation/#p-302671]; *Vṛhaspatitattva*, Ch. 1 (tattvas/yoga), [/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-04d69c].
